name: Burrata Sphere with Basil Oil & Tomato Water Snow
description: A delicate burrata sphere paired with aromatic basil oil and refreshing tomato water snow, combining creamy, herbal, and acidic elements.
keywords: burrata, basil oil, tomato water, molecular gastronomy, modernist, cold dish

winePairings.name: Rosé from Provence
winePairings.notes: Crisp strawberry + herb notes match basil/tomato.

steps.instruction: Spoon a small pool of basil oil on the plate. Place the burrata sphere in the center.
Grate tomato water snow over and around the sphere right before serving.
Garnish with micro basil leaves, cracked black pepper, or a drizzle of olive oil. Serve immediately.

ingredients.note: 1 g sodium alginate per 500 mL water
5 g calcium lactate per 1 L water

steps.instruction: Open the burrata and remove the stracciatella filling.
Blend the stracciatella, cream, salt, and sodium alginate until completely smooth.
Using a round spoon or mold, form small portions and drop into the calcium lactate bath for about 2 minutes.
Rinse gently in cold water to remove any excess calcium.
Set aside in a little olive oil to prevent sticking.
Alternative: Skip steps 1-6 and use the burrata as is for a simpler presentation with similar flavor.

ingredients.item: Neutral oil (grapeseed or light olive oil)

steps.instruction: Blanch basil leaves in boiling water for 10 seconds, then shock in ice water.
Squeeze dry, then blend with oil and salt until bright green.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Blend tomatoes with salt and sugar.
Line a bowl with cheesecloth, pour the purée in, and let it drip overnight in the fridge to collect tomato water.
Mix tomato water with Xanthan gum and freeze tomato water solid.
